feedback.issue:
**Q: What should "top right corner" actually mean, given there's no viewport-spanning top bar today? (a) Add a new slim top bar across the top of the main content area with the toggle at its far right; (b) keep it in the left sidebar's top-right (roughly where it is now); or (c) place it in each page's existing header row, top-right.**

A: Lets actually put it near the sign out button instead

**Q: Which layouts should change? The toggle also appears on the sign-in/auth screen (no sidebar), currently centered next to the "Pipeliner" heading.**

A: None

**Q: If a new top bar is added, should it hold anything besides the toggle (e.g. breadcrumbs, page title, or the "Sign out" control now at the bottom of the sidebar)?**

A: No top bar

**Q: On pages whose header already has a top-right primary action (New Project, New Pipeline, etc.), is a single shared top bar sitting above those page headers acceptable so the toggle and the primary action don't compete in the same row?**

A: No top bar
